notes: Pi is given a slow count-to-10 prompt; while it is mid-turn the driver types a follow-up that pi QUEUES (Enter steers/queues; the buffered text is consumed after the in-flight turn settles). KEY ASSERTION: the queued user line introduces NO extra state boundary into the in-flight turn — the parser (core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go) only flips working→ready on stopReason=="stop", and a queued `user` line is classified user_message (no turn boundary), so the first turn's working span stays contiguous through the queued arrival and never flickers to ready or waiting. AGENT-SPECIFIC (vs claudecode/codex): pi does NOT fold the queued follow-up into the in-flight turn as one contiguous span; it consumes the queued message as the NEXT turn after the current settles. So the observable is TWO working→ready arcs on ONE rollout session_id — the in-flight turn (q1) then the queued message's own arc (q2) — NOT one continuous span. The spec asserts: q1's working span is contiguous (no ready/waiting mid-turn, no turn boundary on the queued arrival), q1 settles to ready, then q2 (the queued message) opens its own working→ready arc on the same session, no waiting anywhere, a single process_exited at teardown, final state ready. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as pi/session-start, pi/multi-turn-conversation). The TURN ARCS must all land on the rollout session: q1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (necessarily the rollout session; the pre-session never goes working); every later turn phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to q1_start's session via same_session_as. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, sub-second timing, and the precise interleave of the queued user line vs the count vary (model-output + queue-timing variance); pi may also emit MORE than two working→ready arcs if the slow count produces multiple activity bursts — the spec asserts the first arc is contiguous and the queued message yields a clean follow-on arc with no waiting, not an exact arc count. Stable structurally: a contiguous q1 working span (no mid-turn ready/waiting), a follow-on q2 working→ready arc on the same rollout session_id, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Slow count-to-10 prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
The in-flight turn completes → back to ready; the working span was contiguous through the queued follow-up's arrival and never entered waiting
Pi consumes the queued follow-up as the next turn → working again on the same rollout session
The queued follow-up's turn completes → back to ready; never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the queued message is consumed → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
